behavior_summary: U8P_LARWEBNS::gotHit is an event handler invoked when the object is struck. It marks a small info/state key on a target, excludes that target from further processing, and then destroys the item instance passed to the handler.
The handler operates locally on the supplied item instance and does not read or write any global flags in the provided excerpt.

The action sequence is: set info (0x20C) on a target derived from the call context, apply a process-exclude to prevent further processing of that target, then call Item::destroy on the item instance parameter.
notable_control_flow: U8P_LARWEBNS::gotHit runs as a short process-style handler: set-info -> process-exclude -> Item::destroy.
The process-exclude step indicates the object/target will be prevented from normal future processing (likely stops further event propagation or AI updates for that instance).
The function returns immediately after destruction; there are no observed loops or additional branches in the excerpt.
open_questions: What exact game-state meaning does info id 0x20C represent? (appears to be a transient marker; exact semantics unknown) [confidence: low]

What precise object is the recipient of the set-info/process-exclude operations (the item parameter itself, its owner, or a different related object)? (likely the passed item but inferred) [confidence: medium]
What is the role of the first parameter (uword) pushed to U8P_LARWEBNS::gotHit? It is present but unused in the snippet. [confidence: medium-low]
